revision.text:
[[ファイル:諏訪地域.png|right|thumb|300px|諏訪地域]] '''諏訪地域'''（すわちいき）は、[[長野県]][[南信地方]]の[[岡谷市]]、[[諏訪市]]、[[茅野市]]を中心とした地域。県を10地域に分ける時に用いられる[https://www.pref.nagano.lg.jp/koho/10koiki/suwa.html 諏訪地域の情報]長野県ホームページ（2018年8月3日閲覧）。旧[[諏訪郡]]全体と一致している。 域内人口は194,439人（[[2019年]]時点）。原村を除く市町で減少傾向にあり、特に[[下諏訪町]]、[[岡谷市]]、[[諏訪市]]では深刻である。3つの市を中心とする複眼構造を為す。また最も人口が多い都市は[[茅野市]]である。 JR[[中央本線]]や[[中央自動車道]]が通っており、[[南アルプス]]を避けて[[関東地方]]や[[山梨県]]と[[東海地方]]を結ぶ交通の要路である。 == 概要 == === 自治体 === 該当する自治体は、西から岡谷市、下諏訪町、諏訪市、[[茅野市]]、[[原村]]、[[富士見町]]である。[[諏訪湖]]を取り巻く西側の「湖周」3市町と、東側の「[[八ヶ岳]]山麓」3市町村に緩やかな連携が見られる。 以下は[[長野県]]の定義である。 * [[岡谷市]] * [[諏訪市]] * [[茅野市]] * [[諏訪郡]]：[[下諏訪町]]、[[富士見町]]、[[原村]] === 地理 === [[ファイル:Suwako2.jpg|250px|thumb|諏訪湖とその周辺（山梨県小淵沢上空より）]] [[東京]]・[[名古屋市|名古屋]]の二大都市圏から約200kmに位置し、[[中央自動車道]]およびJR[[中央本線]]が域内の広域交通を大きく担っている。 地域の北西に諏訪湖があり、東側に[[八ヶ岳連峰]]がそびえる。諏訪湖周辺は[[諏訪盆地]]となっており、人口や産業が集積している。 * 山：[[茶臼山]]、[[八ヶ岳]]、[[蓼科山]]、[[霧ヶ峰]]、三峰山、鷲ヶ峰 * 河川：[[宮川 (天竜川水系)|宮川]]、[[上川 (長野県)|上川]]、中門川、[[角間川]] 、島崎川、衣之渡川、舟渡川、[[砥川　(長野県)|砥川]]、十四瀬川 * 湖沼：[[諏訪湖]]、[[白樺湖]]、 [[蓼科湖]]、 [[鏡湖]]、[[御射鹿池]] === 気候 === 周囲を北東にかけては[[八ヶ岳]]に連なる山々、南西にかけては[[南アルプス]]に連なる山脈に囲まれ、それらの山々と諏訪湖の影響を受ける。[[内陸性気候]]の特色を示す県内にありながら、例えば、春夏秋季の降水量は県内比で多め、冬季の降水量は圧倒的に少ないというように、内陸気候の中でも特徴的な気象環境下にある。気候は、湖周部と八ヶ岳山麓部で異なっている。これは、諏訪湖周辺と八ヶ岳山麓の標高差が大きいことに起因するものである。 避暑地と呼ばれる傾向にあるが、平地に限れば夏場の日中の最高気温は30度前後に達し、それほど涼しくはない。ただし、夜から朝にかけては気温は下がり、過ごしやすい気温になる。[[熱帯夜]]になることはめったにない。 ただし、諏訪地域の気候は標高差の関係から湖周部と八ヶ岳山麓部で異なっている。 諏訪湖周辺では、[[フェーン現象]]の影響を受け、標高が760m近くありながら、夏場には高温になることがある。また、冬は比較的乾燥しており、雪が少ないのが特徴である。また、諏訪湖の影響により、周囲よりも冬季の冷え込みは幾分やわらぐが、全面氷結すると、冷え込みが強まるという特徴がある。八ヶ岳山麓にある茅野市の大部分、原村、富士見町は、標高が800m以上となるため、[[北海道]]並みに夏は涼しく、冬は寒さが厳しい。[[ケッペンの気候区分]]では[[亜寒帯湿潤気候]]に分類される。 === 経済 === 後述する観光以外に、[[精密機械]]を中心に工場が多く[[諏訪圏工業メッセ]]2022年までは[[東洋バルヴ#諏訪工場跡地|諏訪湖イベントホール (旧東バル跡地)]]でおこなわれたが、2023年からは、岡谷市民総合体育館とテクノプラザおかやに変更。[https://suwamesse.jp/ 諏訪圏工業メッセ2023]という[[見本市]]を開催している。これは豊富な[[諏訪湖]]周辺の水を利用して古くから[[製糸業]]が発達したほか、[[第二次世界大戦]]期に関東から[[軍需工場]]が疎開した歴史がある。 == 歴史 == === 古代 === 縄文、弥生時代から、霧ヶ峰を中心に人類の生活の痕跡がある。下諏訪の[[和田峠 (長野県)|和田峠]]を始めとする[[黒曜石]]による[[石器]]、また、[[縄文時代|縄文]]・[[弥生時代]]のものと見られる[[土器]]の破片も採掘される。 ===中世・近世=== [[戦国大名]][[諏訪氏]]によって治められる。代表的人物に[[諏訪頼重 (戦国時代)|諏訪頼重]]がいる。諏訪地域は東南側で[[甲斐国]]（現・[[山梨県]]）に接し、他の[[信濃国|信濃]][[国人]]や甲斐[[武田氏]]との関りから戦乱が度々起きた。諏訪頼重は[[武田信玄]]に攻め込まれ、茅野市[[上原城]]、諏訪市四賀の[[桑原城]]に立てこもったものの敗れ、甲斐にて自害させられている。 その[[従兄弟]]である[[諏訪頼忠]]によって諏訪氏が再興され、息子の[[諏訪頼水]]が[[1600年]]（[[慶長]]5年）の[[関ヶ原の戦い]]における功よって[[諏訪藩|高島藩]]に封じられている。[[江戸時代]]にこの地域を支配していたのは[[高島藩]]で、藩庁は[[高島城]]。以降10代にわたって、諏訪氏により治められた。[[甲州街道]]の[[宿場]]は[[蔦木宿]]、[[金沢宿　(甲州街道)]]、[[上諏訪宿]]と続き、[[下諏訪宿]]は甲州街道の終点で[[中山道]]と交わる下諏訪の手前の[[宿場町]]として栄える。 === 近現代 === * [[1871年]]（明治4年） - [[廃藩置県]]により、[[高島県]]。後[[筑摩県]]に編入。 * [[1875年]]（明治8年） - [[高島城]]天守以下建造物が破脚される。大久保村、柳沢村、八ツ手新田村、払沢新田村、柏木新田村、菖蒲沢村、中新田村、室内新田村が合併し'''原村'''となる。 * [[1876年]]（明治9年） - [[長野県]]編入。また、高島城が高島公園として一般に開放される。 * [[1893年]]（明治26年）[[6月30日]] - 下諏訪村が町制施行。'''下諏訪町'''となる。 * [[1905年]]（明治38年）[[11月25日]] - [[日本国有鉄道|国鉄]]（官設鉄道）[[上諏訪駅]]開業。上諏訪[[車両基地|機関区]]設置。 * [[1936年]]（昭和11年）[[4月1日]] - 諏訪郡平野村、市制を施行し名称変更。'''岡谷市'''が発足。 * [[1941年]]（昭和16年）[[8月10日]] - [[諏訪郡]][[上諏訪町]]、[[豊田村 (長野県諏訪郡)|豊田村]]および[[四賀村 (長野県諏訪郡)|四賀村]]が合併して'''諏訪市'''が誕生する。 * [[1955年]]（昭和30年）[[4月1日]] - [[富士見村 (長野県)|富士見村]]、[[本郷村 (長野県諏訪郡)|本郷村]]、[[境村 (長野県)|境村]]、[[落合村 (長野県)|落合村]]が合併して'''富士見町'''が発足。 * [[1958年]]（昭和33年）[[8月1日]] - 諏訪郡茅野町、市制を施行し'''茅野市'''が発足。 * [[1964年]]（昭和39年）[[3月]] - 松本・諏訪地区が[[新産業都市]]に指定（[[2001年]]失効）。 * [[1970年]]（昭和45年） - 高島城の天守・櫓・門・塀を復元。 * [[1981年]]（昭和56年） - 中央自動車道が開通 == 観光 == 諏訪地域は諏訪湖や八ヶ岳などを抱え、寺社や史跡も多いため、一大観光地となっている。諏訪湖は点在する多数の観光スポットを結びつける役割を持っている。諏訪地域には毎年2000万人近い人が訪れている。 ===寺社・旧跡=== [[ファイル:Takashima castle moat.jpg|right|240px|thumb|高島城復元天守]] 全国的に名を知られている[[諏訪大社]]がある。諏訪大社は以前は「諏訪さま」などと呼ばれていた。諏訪さまへの信仰は[[鎌倉時代]]には既に存在したとされており、[[初詣]]客の数は長野県でトップを誇る。[[信濃国]]の[[一宮]]となっている。温泉寺は[[上諏訪温泉]]のそばの山間にあり、諏訪地域を治めていた高島藩主（諏訪氏）の[[菩提寺]]となっていた。高島城は日本三大湖城の一つで、毎年冬には恒例のすす払いが行われる。[[日本放送協会|NHK]]の大河ドラマ『[[武田信玄 (NHK大河ドラマ)|武田信玄]]』の舞台に使われ放送されていた頃は20万人近くが訪れていたが、現在は4万人程度となってしまっている。諏訪市公式ページに[http://www.city.suwa.nagano.jp/live/ ライブカメラ]が設置されている。 * 諏訪大社 ** 上社 *** 前宮（まえみや）：茅野市 *** 本宮（ほんみや）：諏訪市 ** 下社 *** 春宮（はるみや）：下諏訪町 *** 秋宮（あきみや）：下諏訪町 ** 有名な御柱で駆け下りる場所（木落し坂）：茅野市（上社）・下諏訪町（下社） * 温泉寺：諏訪市 * [[高島城]]：諏訪市 * 儀象堂：下諏訪町 ===美術館・博物館=== 信州は美術館や博物館が多く、諏訪地域も1983年5月に[[北澤美術館本館|北澤美術館]]が開館して以降、質の高い美術館が点在するようになった。[[諏訪市原田泰治美術館]]は地元出身の画家・[[原田泰治]]の個人美術館。名誉館長は友人の歌手、[[さだまさし]]。[[サンリツ服部美術館]]は[[国宝]]「[[楽焼白片身変茶碗]]」（銘不二山／[[本阿弥光悦]]作）を所蔵している。この美術館は[[服部時計店]]3代目社長の[[服部正次]]（はっとりしょうじ、1900 - 1974）と、その長男で[[セイコーエプソン]]社長であった[[服部一郎]]（1932 - 1987）のコレクションを展示するため、[[1995年]]（平成7年）開館した。 *岡谷 ** [[イルフ童画館]] ** [[市立岡谷蚕糸博物館]] ** [[市立岡谷美術考古館]] ** [[小さな絵本美術館]] *下諏訪町 **[[諏訪湖博物館|諏訪湖博物館・赤彦記念館]] ** [[ハーモ美術館]] ** [[SUWAガラスの里]] ** [[ニデックインスツルメンツ|ニデック]]オルゴール記念館すわのね ** しもすわ今昔館おいでや・時計工房・儀象堂 *諏訪市 ** [[北澤美術館本館]] ** [[諏訪市美術館]] ** [[諏訪市博物館]] ** [[諏訪市原田泰治美術館]] ** [[サンリツ服部美術館]] *茅野市 ** [[茅野市尖石縄文考古館]] ** [[茅野市民館#茅野市美術館|茅野市美術館]] ** [[八ヶ岳総合博物館]] **[[山下清]]放浪美術館 **[[康耀堂美術館]] **[[神長官守矢史料館]] *原村 ** [[八ヶ岳美術館]] ** [[八ヶ岳自然文化園]] *富士見町 ** [[井戸尻遺跡|井戸尻考古館]] ** [[富士見町コミュニティプラザ]] === 温泉 === [[ファイル:Kamisuwa sta01bs3200.jpg|thumb|right|220px|上諏訪駅ホームの[[足湯]]]] 諏訪には[[温泉]]も多い。かつて[[上諏訪駅]]には[[プラットホーム]]に温泉があった。温泉施設を持つ駅は他にも例があるが、ホームにあるのはここだけであった。しかし、近年の[[足湯]]ブームによりこの温泉は足湯になってしまった。湯は熱めであるが、足のみを暖める足湯としては一般的な湯加減である。利用者には駅を利用したついでに足湯を楽しむ観光客などが多い。足を暖めることで、血流を良くし、全身を暖める足湯は、[[信濃国|信州]]の[[冬]]の厳しい寒さを凌ぐ目的での入浴にも適している。 * [[上諏訪温泉]]：諏訪市 ** [[片倉館]] * [[下諏訪温泉]]：下諏訪町 * [[奥蓼科温泉郷]]：茅野市 === その他の観光スポット === [[ファイル:Kiri-mine.jpg|220px|thumb|霧ヶ峰]] 霧ヶ峰は[[八ヶ岳中信高原国定公園]]中部にあり、[[フォッサマグナ]]沿いに噴出した[[楯状火山]]である。最高峰は、車山 (1925m) 。[[山地帯]][[夏緑樹林]]と[[亜高山帯]][[針葉樹林]]の境界付近に存在する。[[八島ヶ原湿原]]、踊場湿原からは、[[黒曜石]]を用いた[[石器]]（[[旧石器時代 (日本)|旧石器時代]]のものと推定される）が発見されている。 * [[諏訪湖]]：諏訪市、岡谷市、下諏訪町 * [[日本の音風景100選]]：岡谷市 * [[鳥居平やまびこ公園]]：岡谷市 * [[霧ヶ峰]]：諏訪市、茅野市 * [[白樺湖]]：茅野市 * [[蓼科湖]]：茅野市 === 祭事 === * [[御柱|諏訪大社式年造営御柱大祭]]（寅年、申年の4月、5月） * [[御柱|小宮御柱祭]]（原則寅年、申年の夏〜秋にかけて。一部例外あり） * 諏訪湖まつり（および[[諏訪湖祭湖上花火大会|湖上花火大会]]） *岡谷花火まつり *茅野どんばん *茅野市5000年尖石縄文まつり *[[小津安二郎記念蓼科高原映画祭]] *[[岡谷太鼓祭り]] *御舟祭 *遷座祭 *諏訪よいてこ == 交通 == [[ファイル:長野県の10分割区分.png|thumb|300px|right|{{color|blue|青系色が北信地方（水色：北信地域 青：長野地域）}}
{{color|#990|黄系色が東信地方（黄色：上小地域 薄黄：佐久地域）}}
{{color|red|赤系色が中信地方（オレンジ：大北地域 赤：松本地域） ピンク：木曽地域}}
{{color|green|緑系色が南信地方（黄緑：諏訪地域 抹茶色：上伊那地域 明るい緑：飯伊地域）}}]] === 鉄道 === 域内をJR[[中央本線]]が通っている。西側3市町のうち、駅の利用者数が比較的多い、[[茅野駅]]、[[上諏訪駅]]、[[下諏訪駅]]、[[岡谷駅]]に[[自動改札機]]がある。[[川岸駅]]を除く域内の各駅に簡易Suica改札機が設置されている。 [[普門寺信号場]]から岡谷駅までは、中央東線の本線区間（[[伊藤大八#大八廻り|辰野線]]を除く区間）においては唯一の[[単線]]となっている。その影響で、上下線で最大の輸送量を発揮するダイヤ編成を行ったときに10分おきに電車を発着させるのが精一杯の状況である。なお、中央東線高速化実現期成同盟会によって[[複線化]]についての質問がJR東日本になされているが、[[東日本旅客鉄道|JR東日本]]は現状の輸送力で十分としている。 === 道路 === [[中央自動車道]]、[[長野自動車道]]が通っているほか、[[国道20号]]が中核的な道路になっている。中央自動車道と長野自動車道は[[岡谷ジャンクション]]で接続しており、域内には中央自動車道の[[諏訪南インターチェンジ]]（富士見町）と[[諏訪インターチェンジ]]（諏訪市）、長野自動車道の岡谷IC（岡谷市）が存在する。国道20号は諏訪市内を中心に慢性的な[[交通渋滞]]に悩まされており、数十年前から、茅野市中河原北交差点より中央自動車道諏訪IC付近を経て長野自動車道岡谷ICまでを結ぶ[[諏訪バイパス]]が計画されている。このうち中河原北（茅野市）〜飯島（諏訪市）が暫定供用中、下諏訪岡谷バイパスとして岡谷市内の湖北トンネル南〜岡谷ICが開通しているが、諏訪市、下諏訪町の市街地を主にトンネルで迂回する、最も重要な区間が開通するめどは立っていない。 === 諏訪ナンバー === 従来、諏訪地域の自動車は松本ナンバーを取り付けていたが、知名度の向上による観光でのメリットを目指して2006年10月10日より[[ご当地ナンバー]]を導入。しかし、登録台数が[[総務省]]の求める基準ぎりぎりの水準にとどまっているため、対象地域を拡張するなどの努力目標が課せられている。 == 諏訪地域振興局 == 〒392-8601諏訪市上川1丁目1644番地10 ==脚注・出典== {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == *[[諏訪広域連合]] *[[ピンぼけの家族]] == 外部リンク == *[https://www.pref.nagano.lg.jp/koho/10koiki/suwa.html 諏訪地域の情報] {{長野県の地域}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すわちいき}} [[Category:長野県の地理]] [[Category:諏訪地域|*]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[諏訪地域]] to [[Suwa Region]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
[[ファイル:諏訪地域.png|right|thumb|300px|諏訪地域]] '''諏訪地域'''（すわちいき）は、[[長野県]][[南信地方]]の[[岡谷市]]、[[諏訪市]]、[[茅野市]]を中心とした地域。県を10地域に分ける時に用いられる[https://www.pref.nagano.lg.jp/koho/10koiki/suwa.html 諏訪地域の情報]長野県ホームページ（2018年8月3日閲覧）。旧[[諏訪郡]]全体と一致している。 域内人口は194,439人（[[2019年]]時点）。原村を除く市町で減少傾向にあり、特に[[下諏訪町]]、[[岡谷市]]、[[諏訪市]]では深刻である。3つの市を中心とする複眼構造を為す。また最も人口が多い都市は[[茅野市]]である。 JR[[中央本線]]や[[中央自動車道]]が通っており、[[南アルプス]]を避けて[[関東地方]]や[[山梨県]]と[[東海地方]]を結ぶ交通の要路である。 == 概要 == === 自治体 === 該当する自治体は、西から岡谷市、下諏訪町、諏訪市、[[茅野市]]、[[原村]]、[[富士見町]]である。[[諏訪湖]]を取り巻く西側の「湖周」3市町と、東側の「[[八ヶ岳]]山麓」3市町村に緩やかな連携が見られる。 以下は[[長野県]]の定義である。 * [[岡谷市]] * [[諏訪市]] * [[茅野市]] * [[諏訪郡]]：[[下諏訪町]]、[[富士見町]]、[[原村]] === 地理 === [[ファイル:Suwako2.jpg|250px|thumb|諏訪湖とその周辺（山梨県小淵沢上空より）]] [[東京]]・[[名古屋市|名古屋]]の二大都市圏から約200kmに位置し、[[中央自動車道]]およびJR[[中央本線]]が域内の広域交通を大きく担っている。 地域の北西に諏訪湖があり、東側に[[八ヶ岳連峰]]がそびえる。諏訪湖周辺は[[諏訪盆地]]となっており、人口や産業が集積している。 * 山：[[茶臼山]]、[[八ヶ岳]]、[[蓼科山]]、[[霧ヶ峰]]、三峰山、鷲ヶ峰 * 河川：[[宮川 (天竜川水系)|宮川]]、[[上川 (長野県)|上川]]、中門川、[[角間川]] 、島崎川、衣之渡川、舟渡川、[[砥川　(長野県)|砥川]]、十四瀬川 * 湖沼：[[諏訪湖]]、[[白樺湖]]、 [[蓼科湖]]、 [[鏡湖]]、[[御射鹿池]] === 気候 === 周囲を北東にかけては[[八ヶ岳]]に連なる山々、南西にかけては[[南アルプス]]に連なる山脈に囲まれ、それらの山々と諏訪湖の影響を受ける。[[内陸性気候]]の特色を示す県内にありながら、例えば、春夏秋季の降水量は県内比で多め、冬季の降水量は圧倒的に少ないというように、内陸気候の中でも特徴的な気象環境下にある。気候は、湖周部と八ヶ岳山麓部で異なっている。これは、諏訪湖周辺と八ヶ岳山麓の標高差が大きいことに起因するものである。 避暑地と呼ばれる傾向にあるが、平地に限れば夏場の日中の最高気温は30度前後に達し、それほど涼しくはない。ただし、夜から朝にかけては気温は下がり、過ごしやすい気温になる。[[熱帯夜]]になることはめったにない。 ただし、諏訪地域の気候は標高差の関係から湖周部と八ヶ岳山麓部で異なっている。 諏訪湖周辺では、[[フェーン現象]]の影響を受け、標高が760m近くありながら、夏場には高温になることがある。また、冬は比較的乾燥しており、雪が少ないのが特徴である。また、諏訪湖の影響により、周囲よりも冬季の冷え込みは幾分やわらぐが、全面氷結すると、冷え込みが強まるという特徴がある。八ヶ岳山麓にある茅野市の大部分、原村、富士見町は、標高が800m以上となるため、[[北海道]]並みに夏は涼しく、冬は寒さが厳しい。[[ケッペンの気候区分]]では[[亜寒帯湿潤気候]]に分類される。 === 経済 === 後述する観光以外に、[[精密機械]]を中心に工場が多く[[諏訪圏工業メッセ]]2022年までは[[東洋バルヴ#諏訪工場跡地|諏訪湖イベントホール (旧東バル跡地)]]でおこなわれたが、2023年からは、岡谷市民総合体育館とテクノプラザおかやに変更。[https://suwamesse.jp/ 諏訪圏工業メッセ2023]という[[見本市]]を開催している。これは豊富な[[諏訪湖]]周辺の水を利用して古くから[[製糸業]]が発達したほか、[[第二次世界大戦]]期に関東から[[軍需工場]]が疎開した歴史がある。 == 歴史 == === 古代 === 縄文、弥生時代から、霧ヶ峰を中心に人類の生活の痕跡がある。下諏訪の[[和田峠 (長野県)|和田峠]]を始めとする[[黒曜石]]による[[石器]]、また、[[縄文時代|縄文]]・[[弥生時代]]のものと見られる[[土器]]の破片も採掘される。 ===中世・近世=== [[戦国大名]][[諏訪氏]]によって治められる。代表的人物に[[諏訪頼重 (戦国時代)|諏訪頼重]]がいる。諏訪地域は東南側で[[甲斐国]]（現・[[山梨県]]）に接し、他の[[信濃国|信濃]][[国人]]や甲斐[[武田氏]]との関りから戦乱が度々起きた。諏訪頼重は[[武田信玄]]に攻め込まれ、茅野市[[上原城]]、諏訪市四賀の[[桑原城]]に立てこもったものの敗れ、甲斐にて自害させられている。 その[[従兄弟]]である[[諏訪頼忠]]によって諏訪氏が再興され、息子の[[諏訪頼水]]が[[1600年]]（[[慶長]]5年）の[[関ヶ原の戦い]]における功よって[[諏訪藩|高島藩]]に封じられている。[[江戸時代]]にこの地域を支配していたのは[[高島藩]]で、藩庁は[[高島城]]。以降10代にわたって、諏訪氏により治められた。[[甲州街道]]の[[宿場]]は[[蔦木宿]]、[[金沢宿　(甲州街道)]]、[[上諏訪宿]]と続き、[[下諏訪宿]]は甲州街道の終点で[[中山道]]と交わる下諏訪の手前の[[宿場町]]として栄える。 === 近現代 === * [[1871年]]（明治4年） - [[廃藩置県]]により、[[高島県]]。後[[筑摩県]]に編入。 * [[1875年]]（明治8年） - [[高島城]]天守以下建造物が破脚される。大久保村、柳沢村、八ツ手新田村、払沢新田村、柏木新田村、菖蒲沢村、中新田村、室内新田村が合併し'''原村'''となる。 * [[1876年]]（明治9年） - [[長野県]]編入。また、高島城が高島公園として一般に開放される。 * [[1893年]]（明治26年）[[6月30日]] - 下諏訪村が町制施行。'''下諏訪町'''となる。 * [[1905年]]（明治38年）[[11月25日]] - [[日本国有鉄道|国鉄]]（官設鉄道）[[上諏訪駅]]開業。上諏訪[[車両基地|機関区]]設置。 * [[1936年]]（昭和11年）[[4月1日]] - 諏訪郡平野村、市制を施行し名称変更。'''岡谷市'''が発足。 * [[1941年]]（昭和16年）[[8月10日]] - [[諏訪郡]][[上諏訪町]]、[[豊田村 (長野県諏訪郡)|豊田村]]および[[四賀村 (長野県諏訪郡)|四賀村]]が合併して'''諏訪市'''が誕生する。 * [[1955年]]（昭和30年）[[4月1日]] - [[富士見村 (長野県)|富士見村]]、[[本郷村 (長野県諏訪郡)|本郷村]]、[[境村 (長野県)|境村]]、[[落合村 (長野県)|落合村]]が合併して'''富士見町'''が発足。 * [[1958年]]（昭和33年）[[8月1日]] - 諏訪郡茅野町、市制を施行し'''茅野市'''が発足。 * [[1964年]]（昭和39年）[[3月]] - 松本・諏訪地区が[[新産業都市]]に指定（[[2001年]]失効）。 * [[1970年]]（昭和45年） - 高島城の天守・櫓・門・塀を復元。 * [[1981年]]（昭和56年） - 中央自動車道が開通 == 観光 == 諏訪地域は諏訪湖や八ヶ岳などを抱え、寺社や史跡も多いため、一大観光地となっている。諏訪湖は点在する多数の観光スポットを結びつける役割を持っている。諏訪地域には毎年2000万人近い人が訪れている。 ===寺社・旧跡=== [[ファイル:Takashima castle moat.jpg|right|240px|thumb|高島城復元天守]] 全国的に名を知られている[[諏訪大社]]がある。諏訪大社は以前は「諏訪さま」などと呼ばれていた。諏訪さまへの信仰は[[鎌倉時代]]には既に存在したとされており、[[初詣]]客の数は長野県でトップを誇る。[[信濃国]]の[[一宮]]となっている。温泉寺は[[上諏訪温泉]]のそばの山間にあり、諏訪地域を治めていた高島藩主（諏訪氏）の[[菩提寺]]となっていた。高島城は日本三大湖城の一つで、毎年冬には恒例のすす払いが行われる。[[日本放送協会|NHK]]の大河ドラマ『[[武田信玄 (NHK大河ドラマ)|武田信玄]]』の舞台に使われ放送されていた頃は20万人近くが訪れていたが、現在は4万人程度となってしまっている。諏訪市公式ページに[http://www.city.suwa.nagano.jp/live/ ライブカメラ]が設置されている。 * 諏訪大社 ** 上社 *** 前宮（まえみや）：茅野市 *** 本宮（ほんみや）：諏訪市 ** 下社 *** 春宮（はるみや）：下諏訪町 *** 秋宮（あきみや）：下諏訪町 ** 有名な御柱で駆け下りる場所（木落し坂）：茅野市（上社）・下諏訪町（下社） * 温泉寺：諏訪市 * [[高島城]]：諏訪市 * 儀象堂：下諏訪町 ===美術館・博物館=== 信州は美術館や博物館が多く、諏訪地域も1983年5月に[[北澤美術館本館|北澤美術館]]が開館して以降、質の高い美術館が点在するようになった。[[諏訪市原田泰治美術館]]は地元出身の画家・[[原田泰治]]の個人美術館。名誉館長は友人の歌手、[[さだまさし]]。[[サンリツ服部美術館]]は[[国宝]]「[[楽焼白片身変茶碗]]」（銘不二山／[[本阿弥光悦]]作）を所蔵している。この美術館は[[服部時計店]]3代目社長の[[服部正次]]（はっとりしょうじ、1900 - 1974）と、その長男で[[セイコーエプソン]]社長であった[[服部一郎]]（1932 - 1987）のコレクションを展示するため、[[1995年]]（平成7年）開館した。 *岡谷 ** [[イルフ童画館]] ** [[市立岡谷蚕糸博物館]] ** [[市立岡谷美術考古館]] ** [[小さな絵本美術館]] *下諏訪町 **[[諏訪湖博物館|諏訪湖博物館・赤彦記念館]] ** [[ハーモ美術館]] ** [[SUWAガラスの里]] ** [[ニデックインスツルメンツ|ニデック]]オルゴール記念館すわのね ** しもすわ今昔館おいでや・時計工房・儀象堂 *諏訪市 ** [[北澤美術館本館]] ** [[諏訪市美術館]] ** [[諏訪市博物館]] ** [[諏訪市原田泰治美術館]] ** [[サンリツ服部美術館]] *茅野市 ** [[茅野市尖石縄文考古館]] ** [[茅野市民館#茅野市美術館|茅野市美術館]] ** [[八ヶ岳総合博物館]] **[[山下清]]放浪美術館 **[[康耀堂美術館]] **[[神長官守矢史料館]] *原村 ** [[八ヶ岳美術館]] ** [[八ヶ岳自然文化園]] *富士見町 ** [[井戸尻遺跡|井戸尻考古館]] ** [[富士見町コミュニティプラザ]] === 温泉 === [[ファイル:Kamisuwa sta01bs3200.jpg|thumb|right|220px|上諏訪駅ホームの[[足湯]]]] 諏訪には[[温泉]]も多い。かつて[[上諏訪駅]]には[[プラットホーム]]に温泉があった。温泉施設を持つ駅は他にも例があるが、ホームにあるのはここだけであった。しかし、近年の[[足湯]]ブームによりこの温泉は足湯になってしまった。湯は熱めであるが、足のみを暖める足湯としては一般的な湯加減である。利用者には駅を利用したついでに足湯を楽しむ観光客などが多い。足を暖めることで、血流を良くし、全身を暖める足湯は、[[信濃国|信州]]の[[冬]]の厳しい寒さを凌ぐ目的での入浴にも適している。 * [[上諏訪温泉]]：諏訪市 ** [[片倉館]] * [[下諏訪温泉]]：下諏訪町 * [[奥蓼科温泉郷]]：茅野市 === その他の観光スポット === [[ファイル:Kiri-mine.jpg|220px|thumb|霧ヶ峰]] 霧ヶ峰は[[八ヶ岳中信高原国定公園]]中部にあり、[[フォッサマグナ]]沿いに噴出した[[楯状火山]]である。最高峰は、車山 (1925m) 。[[山地帯]][[夏緑樹林]]と[[亜高山帯]][[針葉樹林]]の境界付近に存在する。[[八島ヶ原湿原]]、踊場湿原からは、[[黒曜石]]を用いた[[石器]]（[[旧石器時代 (日本)|旧石器時代]]のものと推定される）が発見されている。 * [[諏訪湖]]：諏訪市、岡谷市、下諏訪町 * [[日本の音風景100選]]：岡谷市 * [[鳥居平やまびこ公園]]：岡谷市 * [[霧ヶ峰]]：諏訪市、茅野市 * [[白樺湖]]：茅野市 * [[蓼科湖]]：茅野市 === 祭事 === * [[御柱|諏訪大社式年造営御柱大祭]]（寅年、申年の4月、5月） * [[御柱|小宮御柱祭]]（原則寅年、申年の夏〜秋にかけて。一部例外あり） * 諏訪湖まつり（および[[諏訪湖祭湖上花火大会|湖上花火大会]]） *岡谷花火まつり *茅野どんばん *茅野市5000年尖石縄文まつり *[[小津安二郎記念蓼科高原映画祭]] *[[岡谷太鼓祭り]] *御舟祭 *遷座祭 *諏訪よいてこ == 交通 == [[ファイル:長野県の10分割区分.png|thumb|300px|right|{{color|blue|青系色が北信地方（水色：北信地域 青：長野地域）}}
{{color|#990|黄系色が東信地方（黄色：上小地域 薄黄：佐久地域）}}
{{color|red|赤系色が中信地方（オレンジ：大北地域 赤：松本地域） ピンク：木曽地域}}
{{color|green|緑系色が南信地方（黄緑：諏訪地域 抹茶色：上伊那地域 明るい緑：飯伊地域）}}]] === 鉄道 === 域内をJR[[中央本線]]が通っている。西側3市町のうち、駅の利用者数が比較的多い、[[茅野駅]]、[[上諏訪駅]]、[[下諏訪駅]]、[[岡谷駅]]に[[自動改札機]]がある。[[川岸駅]]を除く域内の各駅に簡易Suica改札機が設置されている。 [[普門寺信号場]]から岡谷駅までは、中央東線の本線区間（[[伊藤大八#大八廻り|辰野線]]を除く区間）においては唯一の[[単線]]となっている。その影響で、上下線で最大の輸送量を発揮するダイヤ編成を行ったときに10分おきに電車を発着させるのが精一杯の状況である。なお、中央東線高速化実現期成同盟会によって[[複線化]]についての質問がJR東日本になされているが、[[東日本旅客鉄道|JR東日本]]は現状の輸送力で十分としている。 === 道路 === [[中央自動車道]]、[[長野自動車道]]が通っているほか、[[国道20号]]が中核的な道路になっている。中央自動車道と長野自動車道は[[岡谷ジャンクション]]で接続しており、域内には中央自動車道の[[諏訪南インターチェンジ]]（富士見町）と[[諏訪インターチェンジ]]（諏訪市）、長野自動車道の岡谷IC（岡谷市）が存在する。国道20号は諏訪市内を中心に慢性的な[[交通渋滞]]に悩まされており、数十年前から、茅野市中河原北交差点より中央自動車道諏訪IC付近を経て長野自動車道岡谷ICまでを結ぶ[[諏訪バイパス]]が計画されている。このうち中河原北（茅野市）〜飯島（諏訪市）が暫定供用中、下諏訪岡谷バイパスとして岡谷市内の湖北トンネル南〜岡谷ICが開通しているが、諏訪市、下諏訪町の市街地を主にトンネルで迂回する、最も重要な区間が開通するめどは立っていない。 === 諏訪ナンバー === 従来、諏訪地域の自動車は松本ナンバーを取り付けていたが、知名度の向上による観光でのメリットを目指して2006年10月10日より[[ご当地ナンバー]]を導入。しかし、登録台数が[[総務省]]の求める基準ぎりぎりの水準にとどまっているため、対象地域を拡張するなどの努力目標が課せられている。 == 諏訪地域振興局 == 〒392-8601諏訪市上川1丁目1644番地10 ==脚注・出典== {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == *[[諏訪広域連合]] *[[ピンぼけの家族]] == 外部リンク == *[https://www.pref.nagano.lg.jp/koho/10koiki/suwa.html 諏訪地域の情報] {{長野県の地域}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すわちいき}} [[Category:長野県の地理]] [[Category:諏訪地域|*]]

revision.text:
{{Unreferenced|date=October 2019}}
'''Suwa Region''' (諏訪地域) refers to an area in the southern part of [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]'s Minami-Shin region, centered around the cities of [[:en:Okaya, Nagano|Okaya]], [[:en:Suwa, Nagano|Suwa]], and [[:en:Chino, Nagano|Chino]]. It is one of the ten administrative regions designated by the prefecture.<ref>[https://www.pref.nagano.lg.jp/koho/10koiki/suwa.html Information on the Suwa Region], Nagano Prefecture website (accessed August 3, 2018).</ref> This area corresponds closely to the former [[:en:Suwa District, Nagano|Suwa District]].
As of 2019, the region's population was 194,439. Except for Hara Village, most municipalities in the area are experiencing a population decline, with particularly severe decreases in [[:en:Shimosuwa|Shimosuwa]], Okaya City, and Suwa City. The region is characterized by a multi-core structure centered on its three main cities, with Chino City being the most populous.
The [[:en:Chūō Main Line|Chūō Main Line]] and [[:en:Chūō Expressway|Chūō Expressway]] pass through the area, serving as key transport routes connecting [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō Region]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], and the [[:en:Tōkai region|Tōkai Region]] while avoiding the Southern Alps.
== Overview == === Municipalities === The Suwa Region encompasses the following municipalities, stretching from west to east: * [[:en:Okaya, Nagano|Okaya]] * [[:en:Suwa, Nagano|Suwa]] * [[:en:Chino, Nagano|Chino]] * Suwa District: ** [[:en:Shimosuwa|Shimosuwa]] ** [[:en:Fujimi, Nagano|Fujimi]] ** [[:en:Hara, Nagano|Hara]]
The region can be broadly divided into two areas: - The "Lake Area" (west side) encompassing the three municipalities surrounding [[:en:Lake Suwa|Lake Suwa]]. - The "Yatsugatake Foothills" (east side), including Chino City, Hara Village, and Fujimi Town.
=== Geography === The Suwa Region is located approximately 200 km from Tokyo and Nagoya, connected by the Chūō Expressway and the JR Chūō Main Line. * '''Mountains''': [[:en:Mount Tateshina|Mount Tateshina]], [[:en:Southern Yatsugatake Volcanic Group|Yatsugatake]], [[:en:Mount Kirigamine|Mount Kirigamine]] * '''Rivers''': Miyagawa River, Kamikawa River, Nakamongawa River * '''Lakes''': Lake Suwa, {{ill|Lake Shirakaba|ja|白樺湖|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Shirakaba|qq=}}, {{ill|Lake Tateshina|ja|蓼科湖|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Tateshina|qq=}}
=== Climate === The region's climate varies significantly due to its topography. Areas near Lake Suwa tend to experience warmer summers and drier winters due to the Foehn effect, while the Yatsugatake Foothills are cooler in summer and have harsher winters, with climates akin to those of Hokkaido.
=== Economy === The Suwa Region has a history of silk production, later transitioning to a precision machinery industry. The {{ill|Suwa Area Industrial Messe|ja|諏訪圏工業メッセ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Area Industrial Messe|qq=}}, an industrial trade fair, highlights the area's manufacturing capabilities.
== History == === Ancient === The region's human activity dates back to the Jomon and Yayoi periods, with artifacts such as obsidian tools found at sites like Wada Pass.
=== Medieval to Early Modern Period === During the Sengoku period, the Suwa clan governed the region, with prominent figures like [[:en:Suwa Yorishige|Suwa Yorishige]]. After various conflicts, the clan was re-established under Suwa Yorimoto and later served the Tokugawa shogunate as lords of the Takashima Domain.
=== Modern === - 1871: Abolition of the domain system; incorporated into Takashima Prefecture and later Nagano Prefecture. - 1981: Chūō Expressway opens, improving regional connectivity.
== Tourism == The Suwa Region is a prominent tourist destination, drawing millions annually with attractions such as: - [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]], a nationally renowned Shinto shrine complex. - [[:en:Takashima Castle|Takashima Castle]], known as one of Japan’s three famous lakeside castles. - Numerous hot springs, including {{ill|Kamisuwa Onsen|ja|上諏訪温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisuwa Onsen|qq=}} and {{ill|Shimosuwa Onsen|ja|下諏訪温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosuwa Onsen|qq=}}.
=== Museums and Galleries === - {{ill|Kitazawa Museum of Art|ja|北澤美術館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitazawa Museum of Art|qq=}} - [[:en:Togariishi Museum of Jōmon Archaeology|Chino City Tateshina Archaeological Museum]]
=== Natural Attractions === - [[:en:Lake Suwa|Lake Suwa]] - [[:en:Mount Kirigamine|Mount Kirigamine]]
== Transportation == * '''Rail''': JR Chūō Main Line, with major stations such as Kamisuwa and Chino. * '''Road''': Chūō Expressway and Route 20, serving as primary thoroughfares.
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Suwa Area Federation|ja|諏訪広域連合|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Area Federation|qq=}}
== External Links == * [https://www.pref.nagano.lg.jp/koho/10koiki/suwa.html Official Suwa Region Information]
{{Region of Nagano Prefecture}}
{{translated page|ja|諏訪地域|version=101280331|comment=Imported full ja history}}

[[Category:Geography of Nagano Prefecture]]
}}

== Tourism == The Suwa Region is a prominent tourist destination, drawing millions annually with attractions such as: - [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]], a nationally renowned Shinto shrine complex. - [[:en:Takashima Castle|Takashima Castle]], known as one of Japan’s three famous lakeside castles. - Numerous hot springs, including {{ill|Kamisuwa Onsen|ja|上諏訪温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisuwa Onsen|qq=}} and {{ill|Shimosuwa Onsen|ja|下諏訪温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosuwa Onsen|qq=}}.
=== Museums and Galleries === - {{ill|Kitazawa Museum of Art|ja|北澤美術館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitazawa Museum of Art|qq=}} - [[:en:Togariishi Museum of Jōmon Archaeology|Chino City Tateshina Archaeological Museum]]
=== Natural Attractions === - [[:en:Lake Suwa|Lake Suwa]] - [[:en:Mount Kirigamine|Mount Kirigamine]]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11631849]] Wikidata name: Suwa area [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=October 2019}}
'''Suwa Region''' (諏訪地域) refers to an area in the southern part of [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]'s Minami-Shin region, centered around the cities of [[:en:Okaya, Nagano|Okaya]], [[:en:Suwa, Nagano|Suwa]], and [[:en:Chino, Nagano|Chino]]. It is one of the ten administrative regions designated by the prefecture.<ref>[https://www.pref.nagano.lg.jp/koho/10koiki/suwa.html Information on the Suwa Region], Nagano Prefecture website (accessed August 3, 2018).</ref> This area corresponds closely to the former [[:en:Suwa District, Nagano|Suwa District]].
As of 2019, the region's population was 194,439. Except for Hara Village, most municipalities in the area are experiencing a population decline, with particularly severe decreases in [[:en:Shimosuwa|Shimosuwa]], Okaya City, and Suwa City. The region is characterized by a multi-core structure centered on its three main cities, with Chino City being the most populous.
The [[:en:Chūō Main Line|Chūō Main Line]] and [[:en:Chūō Expressway|Chūō Expressway]] pass through the area, serving as key transport routes connecting [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō Region]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], and the [[:en:Tōkai region|Tōkai Region]] while avoiding the Southern Alps.
== Overview == === Municipalities === The Suwa Region encompasses the following municipalities, stretching from west to east: * [[:en:Okaya, Nagano|Okaya]] * [[:en:Suwa, Nagano|Suwa]] * [[:en:Chino, Nagano|Chino]] * Suwa District: ** [[:en:Shimosuwa|Shimosuwa]] ** [[:en:Fujimi, Nagano|Fujimi]] ** [[:en:Hara, Nagano|Hara]]
The region can be broadly divided into two areas: - The "Lake Area" (west side) encompassing the three municipalities surrounding [[:en:Lake Suwa|Lake Suwa]]. - The "Yatsugatake Foothills" (east side), including Chino City, Hara Village, and Fujimi Town.
=== Geography === The Suwa Region is located approximately 200 km from Tokyo and Nagoya, connected by the Chūō Expressway and the JR Chūō Main Line. * '''Mountains''': [[:en:Mount Tateshina|Mount Tateshina]], [[:en:Southern Yatsugatake Volcanic Group|Yatsugatake]], [[:en:Mount Kirigamine|Mount Kirigamine]] * '''Rivers''': Miyagawa River, Kamikawa River, Nakamongawa River * '''Lakes''': Lake Suwa, {{ill|Lake Shirakaba|ja|白樺湖|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Shirakaba|qq=}}, {{ill|Lake Tateshina|ja|蓼科湖|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Tateshina|qq=}}
=== Climate === The region's climate varies significantly due to its topography. Areas near Lake Suwa tend to experience warmer summers and drier winters due to the Foehn effect, while the Yatsugatake Foothills are cooler in summer and have harsher winters, with climates akin to those of Hokkaido.
=== Economy === The Suwa Region has a history of silk production, later transitioning to a precision machinery industry. The {{ill|Suwa Area Industrial Messe|ja|諏訪圏工業メッセ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Area Industrial Messe|qq=}}, an industrial trade fair, highlights the area's manufacturing capabilities.
== History == === Ancient === The region's human activity dates back to the Jomon and Yayoi periods, with artifacts such as obsidian tools found at sites like Wada Pass.
=== Medieval to Early Modern Period === During the Sengoku period, the Suwa clan governed the region, with prominent figures like [[:en:Suwa Yorishige|Suwa Yorishige]]. After various conflicts, the clan was re-established under Suwa Yorimoto and later served the Tokugawa shogunate as lords of the Takashima Domain.
=== Modern === - 1871: Abolition of the domain system; incorporated into Takashima Prefecture and later Nagano Prefecture. - 1981: Chūō Expressway opens, improving regional connectivity.
== Tourism == The Suwa Region is a prominent tourist destination, drawing millions annually with attractions such as: - [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]], a nationally renowned Shinto shrine complex. - [[:en:Takashima Castle|Takashima Castle]], known as one of Japan’s three famous lakeside castles. - Numerous hot springs, including {{ill|Kamisuwa Onsen|ja|上諏訪温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisuwa Onsen|qq=}} and {{ill|Shimosuwa Onsen|ja|下諏訪温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosuwa Onsen|qq=}}.
=== Museums and Galleries === - {{ill|Kitazawa Museum of Art|ja|北澤美術館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitazawa Museum of Art|qq=}} - [[:en:Togariishi Museum of Jōmon Archaeology|Chino City Tateshina Archaeological Museum]]
=== Natural Attractions === - [[:en:Lake Suwa|Lake Suwa]] - [[:en:Mount Kirigamine|Mount Kirigamine]]
== Transportation == * '''Rail''': JR Chūō Main Line, with major stations such as Kamisuwa and Chino. * '''Road''': Chūō Expressway and Route 20, serving as primary thoroughfares.
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Suwa Area Federation|ja|諏訪広域連合|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Area Federation|qq=}}
== External Links == * [https://www.pref.nagano.lg.jp/koho/10koiki/suwa.html Official Suwa Region Information]

== Tourism == The Suwa Region is a prominent tourist destination, drawing millions annually with attractions such as: - [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]], a nationally renowned Shinto shrine complex. - [[:en:Takashima Castle|Takashima Castle]], known as one of Japan’s three famous lakeside castles. - Numerous hot springs, including {{ill|Kamisuwa Onsen|ja|上諏訪温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisuwa Onsen|qq=|1=Kami-Suwa Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11360122}} and {{ill|Shimosuwa Onsen|ja|下諏訪温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosuwa Onsen|qq=|1=Shimosuwa Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361324}}.
=== Museums and Galleries === - {{ill|Kitazawa Museum of Art|ja|北澤美術館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitazawa Museum of Art|qq=|1=Kitazawa Museum of Art|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11403773}} - [[:en:Togariishi Museum of Jōmon Archaeology|Chino City Tateshina Archaeological Museum]]
=== Natural Attractions === - [[:en:Lake Suwa|Lake Suwa]] - [[:en:Mount Kirigamine|Mount Kirigamine]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fa:منطقه سووا]][[ja:諏訪地域]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11631849]] Wikidata name: Suwa area [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=October 2019}}
'''Suwa Region''' (諏訪地域) refers to an area in the southern part of [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]'s Minami-Shin region, centered around the cities of [[:en:Okaya, Nagano|Okaya]], [[:en:Suwa, Nagano|Suwa]], and [[:en:Chino, Nagano|Chino]]. It is one of the ten administrative regions designated by the prefecture.<ref>[https://www.pref.nagano.lg.jp/koho/10koiki/suwa.html Information on the Suwa Region], Nagano Prefecture website (accessed August 3, 2018).</ref> This area corresponds closely to the former [[:en:Suwa District, Nagano|Suwa District]].
As of 2019, the region's population was 194,439. Except for Hara Village, most municipalities in the area are experiencing a population decline, with particularly severe decreases in [[:en:Shimosuwa|Shimosuwa]], Okaya City, and Suwa City. The region is characterized by a multi-core structure centered on its three main cities, with Chino City being the most populous.
The [[:en:Chūō Main Line|Chūō Main Line]] and [[:en:Chūō Expressway|Chūō Expressway]] pass through the area, serving as key transport routes connecting [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō Region]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], and the [[:en:Tōkai region|Tōkai Region]] while avoiding the Southern Alps.
== Overview == === Municipalities === The Suwa Region encompasses the following municipalities, stretching from west to east: * [[:en:Okaya, Nagano|Okaya]] * [[:en:Suwa, Nagano|Suwa]] * [[:en:Chino, Nagano|Chino]] * Suwa District: ** [[:en:Shimosuwa|Shimosuwa]] ** [[:en:Fujimi, Nagano|Fujimi]] ** [[:en:Hara, Nagano|Hara]]
The region can be broadly divided into two areas: - The "Lake Area" (west side) encompassing the three municipalities surrounding [[:en:Lake Suwa|Lake Suwa]]. - The "Yatsugatake Foothills" (east side), including Chino City, Hara Village, and Fujimi Town.
=== Geography === The Suwa Region is located approximately 200 km from Tokyo and Nagoya, connected by the Chūō Expressway and the JR Chūō Main Line. * '''Mountains''': [[:en:Mount Tateshina|Mount Tateshina]], [[:en:Southern Yatsugatake Volcanic Group|Yatsugatake]], [[:en:Mount Kirigamine|Mount Kirigamine]] * '''Rivers''': Miyagawa River, Kamikawa River, Nakamongawa River * '''Lakes''': Lake Suwa, {{ill|Lake Shirakaba|ja|白樺湖|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Shirakaba|1=Lake Shirakaba|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579886}}, {{ill|Lake Tateshina|ja|蓼科湖|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Tateshina|1=Lake Tateshina|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621788}}
=== Climate === The region's climate varies significantly due to its topography. Areas near Lake Suwa tend to experience warmer summers and drier winters due to the Foehn effect, while the Yatsugatake Foothills are cooler in summer and have harsher winters, with climates akin to those of Hokkaido.
=== Economy === The Suwa Region has a history of silk production, later transitioning to a precision machinery industry. The {{ill|Suwa Area Industrial Messe|ja|諏訪圏工業メッセ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Area Industrial Messe|qq=}}, an industrial trade fair, highlights the area's manufacturing capabilities.
== History == === Ancient === The region's human activity dates back to the Jomon and Yayoi periods, with artifacts such as obsidian tools found at sites like Wada Pass.
=== Medieval to Early Modern Period === During the Sengoku period, the Suwa clan governed the region, with prominent figures like [[:en:Suwa Yorishige|Suwa Yorishige]]. After various conflicts, the clan was re-established under Suwa Yorimoto and later served the Tokugawa shogunate as lords of the Takashima Domain.
=== Modern === - 1871: Abolition of the domain system; incorporated into Takashima Prefecture and later Nagano Prefecture. - 1981: Chūō Expressway opens, improving regional connectivity.
== Tourism == The Suwa Region is a prominent tourist destination, drawing millions annually with attractions such as: - [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]], a nationally renowned Shinto shrine complex. - [[:en:Takashima Castle|Takashima Castle]], known as one of Japan’s three famous lakeside castles. - Numerous hot springs, including {{ill|Kamisuwa Onsen|ja|上諏訪温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisuwa Onsen|1=Kami-Suwa Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11360122}} and {{ill|Shimosuwa Onsen|ja|下諏訪温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosuwa Onsen|1=Shimosuwa Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361324}}.
=== Museums and Galleries === - {{ill|Kitazawa Museum of Art|ja|北澤美術館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitazawa Museum of Art|1=Kitazawa Museum of Art|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11403773}} - [[:en:Togariishi Museum of Jōmon Archaeology|Chino City Tateshina Archaeological Museum]]
=== Natural Attractions === - [[:en:Lake Suwa|Lake Suwa]] - [[:en:Mount Kirigamine|Mount Kirigamine]]
== Transportation == * '''Rail''': JR Chūō Main Line, with major stations such as Kamisuwa and Chino. * '''Road''': Chūō Expressway and Route 20, serving as primary thoroughfares.

=== Medieval to Early Modern Period === During the Sengoku period, the Suwa clan governed the region, with prominent figures like [[:en:Suwa Yorishige|Suwa Yorishige]]. After various conflicts, the clan was re-established under Suwa Yorimoto and later served the Tokugawa shogunate as lords of the Takashima Domain.
=== Modern === - 1871: Abolition of the domain system; incorporated into Takashima Prefecture and later Nagano Prefecture. - 1981: Chūō Expressway opens, improving regional connectivity.
== Tourism == The Suwa Region is a prominent tourist destination, drawing millions annually with attractions such as: - [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]], a nationally renowned Shinto shrine complex. - [[:en:Takashima Castle|Takashima Castle]], known as one of Japan’s three famous lakeside castles. - Numerous hot springs, including {{ill|Kamisuwa Onsen|ja|上諏訪温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisuwa Onsen|1=Kami-Suwa Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11360122}} and {{ill|Shimosuwa Onsen|ja|下諏訪温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosuwa Onsen|1=Shimosuwa Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361324}}.
=== Museums and Galleries === - {{ill|Kitazawa Museum of Art|ja|北澤美術館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitazawa Museum of Art|1=Kitazawa Museum of Art|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11403773}} - [[:en:Togariishi Museum of Jōmon Archaeology|Chino City Tateshina Archaeological Museum]]
=== Natural Attractions === - [[:en:Lake Suwa|Lake Suwa]] - [[:en:Mount Kirigamine|Mount Kirigamine]]
== Transportation == * '''Rail''': JR Chūō Main Line, with major stations such as Kamisuwa and Chino. * '''Road''': Chūō Expressway and Route 20, serving as primary thoroughfares.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fa:منطقه سووا]][[ja:諏訪地域]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11631849]] Wikidata name: Suwa area [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=October 2019}}
'''Suwa Region''' (諏訪地域) refers to an area in the southern part of [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]'s Minami-Shin region, centered around the cities of [[:en:Okaya, Nagano|Okaya]], [[:en:Suwa, Nagano|Suwa]], and [[:en:Chino, Nagano|Chino]]. It is one of the ten administrative regions designated by the prefecture.<ref>[https://www.pref.nagano.lg.jp/koho/10koiki/suwa.html Information on the Suwa Region], Nagano Prefecture website (accessed August 3, 2018).</ref> This area corresponds closely to the former [[:en:Suwa District, Nagano|Suwa District]].
As of 2019, the region's population was 194,439. Except for Hara Village, most municipalities in the area are experiencing a population decline, with particularly severe decreases in [[:en:Shimosuwa|Shimosuwa]], Okaya City, and Suwa City. The region is characterized by a multi-core structure centered on its three main cities, with Chino City being the most populous.
The [[:en:Chūō Main Line|Chūō Main Line]] and [[:en:Chūō Expressway|Chūō Expressway]] pass through the area, serving as key transport routes connecting [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō Region]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], and the [[:en:Tōkai region|Tōkai Region]] while avoiding the Southern Alps.
== Overview == === Municipalities === The Suwa Region encompasses the following municipalities, stretching from west to east: * [[:en:Okaya, Nagano|Okaya]] * [[:en:Suwa, Nagano|Suwa]] * [[:en:Chino, Nagano|Chino]] * Suwa District: ** [[:en:Shimosuwa|Shimosuwa]] ** [[:en:Fujimi, Nagano|Fujimi]] ** [[:en:Hara, Nagano|Hara]]
The region can be broadly divided into two areas: - The "Lake Area" (west side) encompassing the three municipalities surrounding [[:en:Lake Suwa|Lake Suwa]]. - The "Yatsugatake Foothills" (east side), including Chino City, Hara Village, and Fujimi Town.
=== Geography === The Suwa Region is located approximately 200 km from Tokyo and Nagoya, connected by the Chūō Expressway and the JR Chūō Main Line. * '''Mountains''': [[:en:Mount Tateshina|Mount Tateshina]], [[:en:Southern Yatsugatake Volcanic Group|Yatsugatake]], [[:en:Mount Kirigamine|Mount Kirigamine]] * '''Rivers''': Miyagawa River, Kamikawa River, Nakamongawa River * '''Lakes''': Lake Suwa, {{ill|Lake Shirakaba|ja|白樺湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Shirakaba|1=Lake Shirakaba|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579886}}, {{ill|Lake Tateshina|ja|蓼科湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Tateshina|1=Lake Tateshina|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621788}}
=== Climate === The region's climate varies significantly due to its topography. Areas near Lake Suwa tend to experience warmer summers and drier winters due to the Foehn effect, while the Yatsugatake Foothills are cooler in summer and have harsher winters, with climates akin to those of Hokkaido.
=== Economy === The Suwa Region has a history of silk production, later transitioning to a precision machinery industry. The {{ill|Suwa Area Industrial Messe|ja|諏訪圏工業メッセ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Area Industrial Messe|qq=}}, an industrial trade fair, highlights the area's manufacturing capabilities.
== History == === Ancient === The region's human activity dates back to the Jomon and Yayoi periods, with artifacts such as obsidian tools found at sites like Wada Pass.
=== Medieval to Early Modern Period === During the Sengoku period, the Suwa clan governed the region, with prominent figures like [[:en:Suwa Yorishige|Suwa Yorishige]]. After various conflicts, the clan was re-established under Suwa Yorimoto and later served the Tokugawa shogunate as lords of the Takashima Domain.
=== Modern === - 1871: Abolition of the domain system; incorporated into Takashima Prefecture and later Nagano Prefecture. - 1981: Chūō Expressway opens, improving regional connectivity.
== Tourism == The Suwa Region is a prominent tourist destination, drawing millions annually with attractions such as: - [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]], a nationally renowned Shinto shrine complex. - [[:en:Takashima Castle|Takashima Castle]], known as one of Japan’s three famous lakeside castles. - Numerous hot springs, including {{ill|Kamisuwa Onsen|ja|上諏訪温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisuwa Onsen|1=Kami-Suwa Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11360122}} and {{ill|Shimosuwa Onsen|ja|下諏訪温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosuwa Onsen|1=Shimosuwa Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361324}}.
=== Museums and Galleries === - {{ill|Kitazawa Museum of Art|ja|北澤美術館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitazawa Museum of Art|1=Kitazawa Museum of Art|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11403773}} - [[:en:Togariishi Museum of Jōmon Archaeology|Chino City Tateshina Archaeological Museum]]
=== Natural Attractions === - [[:en:Lake Suwa|Lake Suwa]] - [[:en:Mount Kirigamine|Mount Kirigamine]]
== Transportation == * '''Rail''': JR Chūō Main Line, with major stations such as Kamisuwa and Chino. * '''Road''': Chūō Expressway and Route 20, serving as primary thoroughfares.
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Suwa Area Federation|ja|諏訪広域連合|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Area Federation|1=Suwa Area Federation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11631871}}

=== Economy === The Suwa Region has a history of silk production, later transitioning to a precision machinery industry. The {{ill|Suwa Area Industrial Messe|ja|諏訪圏工業メッセ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Area Industrial Messe|qq=}}, an industrial trade fair, highlights the area's manufacturing capabilities.
== History == === Ancient === The region's human activity dates back to the Jomon and Yayoi periods, with artifacts such as obsidian tools found at sites like Wada Pass.
=== Medieval to Early Modern Period === During the Sengoku period, the Suwa clan governed the region, with prominent figures like [[:en:Suwa Yorishige|Suwa Yorishige]]. After various conflicts, the clan was re-established under Suwa Yorimoto and later served the Tokugawa shogunate as lords of the Takashima Domain.
=== Modern === - 1871: Abolition of the domain system; incorporated into Takashima Prefecture and later Nagano Prefecture. - 1981: Chūō Expressway opens, improving regional connectivity.
== Tourism == The Suwa Region is a prominent tourist destination, drawing millions annually with attractions such as: - [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]], a nationally renowned Shinto shrine complex. - [[:en:Takashima Castle|Takashima Castle]], known as one of Japan’s three famous lakeside castles. - Numerous hot springs, including {{ill|Kamisuwa Onsen|ja|上諏訪温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisuwa Onsen|1=Kami-Suwa Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11360122}} and {{ill|Shimosuwa Onsen|ja|下諏訪温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosuwa Onsen|1=Shimosuwa Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361324}}.
=== Museums and Galleries === - {{ill|Kitazawa Museum of Art|ja|北澤美術館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitazawa Museum of Art|1=Kitazawa Museum of Art|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11403773}} - [[:en:Togariishi Museum of Jōmon Archaeology|Chino City Tateshina Archaeological Museum]]
=== Natural Attractions === - [[:en:Lake Suwa|Lake Suwa]] - [[:en:Mount Kirigamine|Mount Kirigamine]]
== Transportation == * '''Rail''': JR Chūō Main Line, with major stations such as Kamisuwa and Chino. * '''Road''': Chūō Expressway and Route 20, serving as primary thoroughfares.
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Suwa Area Federation|ja|諏訪広域連合|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Area Federation|1=Suwa Area Federation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11631871}}
== External Links == * [https://www.pref.nagano.lg.jp/koho/10koiki/suwa.html Official Suwa Region Information]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fa:منطقه سووا]][[ja:諏訪地域]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11631849]] Wikidata name: Suwa area [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=October 2019}}
'''Suwa Region''' (諏訪地域) refers to an area in the southern part of [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]'s Minami-Shin region, centered around the cities of [[:en:Okaya, Nagano|Okaya]], [[:en:Suwa, Nagano|Suwa]], and [[:en:Chino, Nagano|Chino]]. It is one of the ten administrative regions designated by the prefecture.<ref>[https://www.pref.nagano.lg.jp/koho/10koiki/suwa.html Information on the Suwa Region], Nagano Prefecture website (accessed August 3, 2018).</ref> This area corresponds closely to the former [[:en:Suwa District, Nagano|Suwa District]].
As of 2019, the region's population was 194,439. Except for Hara Village, most municipalities in the area are experiencing a population decline, with particularly severe decreases in [[:en:Shimosuwa|Shimosuwa]], Okaya City, and Suwa City. The region is characterized by a multi-core structure centered on its three main cities, with Chino City being the most populous.
The [[:en:Chūō Main Line|Chūō Main Line]] and [[:en:Chūō Expressway|Chūō Expressway]] pass through the area, serving as key transport routes connecting [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō Region]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], and the [[:en:Tōkai region|Tōkai Region]] while avoiding the Southern Alps.
== Overview == === Municipalities === The Suwa Region encompasses the following municipalities, stretching from west to east: * [[:en:Okaya, Nagano|Okaya]] * [[:en:Suwa, Nagano|Suwa]] * [[:en:Chino, Nagano|Chino]] * Suwa District: ** [[:en:Shimosuwa|Shimosuwa]] ** [[:en:Fujimi, Nagano|Fujimi]] ** [[:en:Hara, Nagano|Hara]]
The region can be broadly divided into two areas: - The "Lake Area" (west side) encompassing the three municipalities surrounding [[:en:Lake Suwa|Lake Suwa]]. - The "Yatsugatake Foothills" (east side), including Chino City, Hara Village, and Fujimi Town.
=== Geography === The Suwa Region is located approximately 200 km from Tokyo and Nagoya, connected by the Chūō Expressway and the JR Chūō Main Line. * '''Mountains''': [[:en:Mount Tateshina|Mount Tateshina]], [[:en:Southern Yatsugatake Volcanic Group|Yatsugatake]], [[:en:Mount Kirigamine|Mount Kirigamine]] * '''Rivers''': Miyagawa River, Kamikawa River, Nakamongawa River * '''Lakes''': Lake Suwa, {{ill|Lake Shirakaba|ja|白樺湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Shirakaba|1=Lake Shirakaba|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579886}}, {{ill|Lake Tateshina|ja|蓼科湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Tateshina|1=Lake Tateshina|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621788}}
=== Climate === The region's climate varies significantly due to its topography. Areas near Lake Suwa tend to experience warmer summers and drier winters due to the Foehn effect, while the Yatsugatake Foothills are cooler in summer and have harsher winters, with climates akin to those of Hokkaido.
=== Economy === The Suwa Region has a history of silk production, later transitioning to a precision machinery industry. The {{ill|Suwa Area Industrial Messe|ja|諏訪圏工業メッセ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Area Industrial Messe|qq=}}, an industrial trade fair, highlights the area's manufacturing capabilities.
== History == === Ancient === The region's human activity dates back to the Jomon and Yayoi periods, with artifacts such as obsidian tools found at sites like Wada Pass.
=== Medieval to Early Modern Period === During the Sengoku period, the Suwa clan governed the region, with prominent figures like [[:en:Suwa Yorishige|Suwa Yorishige]]. After various conflicts, the clan was re-established under Suwa Yorimoto and later served the Tokugawa shogunate as lords of the Takashima Domain.
=== Modern === - 1871: Abolition of the domain system; incorporated into Takashima Prefecture and later Nagano Prefecture. - 1981: Chūō Expressway opens, improving regional connectivity.
== Tourism == The Suwa Region is a prominent tourist destination, drawing millions annually with attractions such as: - [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]], a nationally renowned Shinto shrine complex. - [[:en:Takashima Castle|Takashima Castle]], known as one of Japan’s three famous lakeside castles. - Numerous hot springs, including {{ill|Kamisuwa Onsen|ja|上諏訪温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisuwa Onsen|1=Kami-Suwa Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11360122}} and {{ill|Shimosuwa Onsen|ja|下諏訪温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosuwa Onsen|1=Shimosuwa Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361324}}.
=== Museums and Galleries === - {{ill|Kitazawa Museum of Art|ja|北澤美術館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitazawa Museum of Art|1=Kitazawa Museum of Art|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11403773}} - [[:en:Togariishi Museum of Jōmon Archaeology|Chino City Tateshina Archaeological Museum]]
=== Natural Attractions === - [[:en:Lake Suwa|Lake Suwa]] - [[:en:Mount Kirigamine|Mount Kirigamine]]
== Transportation == * '''Rail''': JR Chūō Main Line, with major stations such as Kamisuwa and Chino. * '''Road''': Chūō Expressway and Route 20, serving as primary thoroughfares.

The [[:en:Chūō Main Line|Chūō Main Line]] and [[:en:Chūō Expressway|Chūō Expressway]] pass through the area, serving as key transport routes connecting [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō Region]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], and the [[:en:Tōkai region|Tōkai Region]] while avoiding the Southern Alps.
== Overview == === Municipalities === The Suwa Region encompasses the following municipalities, stretching from west to east: * [[:en:Okaya, Nagano|Okaya]] * [[:en:Suwa, Nagano|Suwa]] * [[:en:Chino, Nagano|Chino]] * Suwa District: ** [[:en:Shimosuwa|Shimosuwa]] ** [[:en:Fujimi, Nagano|Fujimi]] ** [[:en:Hara, Nagano|Hara]]
The region can be broadly divided into two areas: - The "Lake Area" (west side) encompassing the three municipalities surrounding [[:en:Lake Suwa|Lake Suwa]]. - The "Yatsugatake Foothills" (east side), including Chino City, Hara Village, and Fujimi Town.
=== Geography === The Suwa Region is located approximately 200 km from Tokyo and Nagoya, connected by the Chūō Expressway and the JR Chūō Main Line. * '''Mountains''': [[:en:Mount Tateshina|Mount Tateshina]], [[:en:Southern Yatsugatake Volcanic Group|Yatsugatake]], [[:en:Mount Kirigamine|Mount Kirigamine]] * '''Rivers''': Miyagawa River, Kamikawa River, Nakamongawa River * '''Lakes''': Lake Suwa, {{ill|Lake Shirakaba|ja|白樺湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Shirakaba|1=Lake Shirakaba|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579886}}, {{ill|Lake Tateshina|ja|蓼科湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Tateshina|1=Lake Tateshina|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621788}}
=== Climate === The region's climate varies significantly due to its topography. Areas near Lake Suwa tend to experience warmer summers and drier winters due to the Foehn effect, while the Yatsugatake Foothills are cooler in summer and have harsher winters, with climates akin to those of Hokkaido.
=== Economy === The Suwa Region has a history of silk production, later transitioning to a precision machinery industry. The {{ill|Suwa Area Industrial Messe|ja|諏訪圏工業メッセ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Area Industrial Messe|qq=}}, an industrial trade fair, highlights the area's manufacturing capabilities.
== History == === Ancient === The region's human activity dates back to the Jomon and Yayoi periods, with artifacts such as obsidian tools found at sites like Wada Pass.
=== Medieval to Early Modern Period === During the Sengoku period, the Suwa clan governed the region, with prominent figures like [[:en:Suwa Yorishige|Suwa Yorishige]]. After various conflicts, the clan was re-established under Suwa Yorimoto and later served the Tokugawa shogunate as lords of the Takashima Domain.
=== Modern === - 1871: Abolition of the domain system; incorporated into Takashima Prefecture and later Nagano Prefecture. - 1981: Chūō Expressway opens, improving regional connectivity.
== Tourism == The Suwa Region is a prominent tourist destination, drawing millions annually with attractions such as: - [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]], a nationally renowned Shinto shrine complex. - [[:en:Takashima Castle|Takashima Castle]], known as one of Japan’s three famous lakeside castles. - Numerous hot springs, including {{ill|Kamisuwa Onsen|ja|上諏訪温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisuwa Onsen|1=Kami-Suwa Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11360122}} and {{ill|Shimosuwa Onsen|ja|下諏訪温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosuwa Onsen|1=Shimosuwa Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361324}}.
=== Museums and Galleries === - {{ill|Kitazawa Museum of Art|ja|北澤美術館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitazawa Museum of Art|1=Kitazawa Museum of Art|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11403773}} - [[:en:Togariishi Museum of Jōmon Archaeology|Chino City Tateshina Archaeological Museum]]
=== Natural Attractions === - [[:en:Lake Suwa|Lake Suwa]] - [[:en:Mount Kirigamine|Mount Kirigamine]]
== Transportation == * '''Rail''': JR Chūō Main Line, with major stations such as Kamisuwa and Chino. * '''Road''': Chūō Expressway and Route 20, serving as primary thoroughfares.
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Suwa Area Federation|ja|諏訪広域連合|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Area Federation|1=Suwa Area Federation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11631871}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fa:منطقه سووا]][[ja:諏訪地域]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fa:منطقه سووا]][[ja:諏訪地域]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fa:منطقه سووا]][[ja:諏訪地域]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11631849]] Wikidata name: Suwa area [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=October 2019}}
'''Suwa Region''' (諏訪地域) refers to an area in the southern part of {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture}}'s Minami-Shin region, centered around the cities of {{ill|Okaya, Nagano|en|Okaya, Nagano|WD=Q838672|lt=Okaya}}, {{ill|Suwa, Nagano|en|Suwa, Nagano|WD=Q846338|lt=Suwa}}, and {{ill|Chino, Nagano|en|Chino, Nagano|WD=Q838660|lt=Chino}}. It is one of the ten administrative regions designated by the prefecture.<ref>[https://www.pref.nagano.lg.jp/koho/10koiki/suwa.html Information on the Suwa Region], Nagano Prefecture website (accessed August 3, 2018).</ref> This area corresponds closely to the former {{ill|Suwa District, Nagano|en|Suwa District, Nagano|WD=Q1131473|lt=Suwa District}}.
As of 2019, the region's population was 194,439. Except for Hara Village, most municipalities in the area are experiencing a population decline, with particularly severe decreases in {{ill|Shimosuwa|en|Shimosuwa|WD=Q1204211|lt=Shimosuwa}}, Okaya City, and Suwa City. The region is characterized by a multi-core structure centered on its three main cities, with Chino City being the most populous.
The {{ill|Chūō Main Line|en|Chūō Main Line|WD=Q1078110|lt=Chūō Main Line}} and {{ill|Chūō Expressway|en|Chūō Expressway|WD=Q1091276|lt=Chūō Expressway}} pass through the area, serving as key transport routes connecting {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō Region}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|en|Yamanashi Prefecture|WD=Q132720|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture}}, and the {{ill|Tōkai region|en|Tōkai region|WD=Q398989|lt=Tōkai Region}} while avoiding the Southern Alps.
== Overview == === Municipalities === The Suwa Region encompasses the following municipalities, stretching from west to east: * {{ill|Okaya, Nagano|en|Okaya, Nagano|WD=Q838672|lt=Okaya}} * {{ill|Suwa, Nagano|en|Suwa, Nagano|WD=Q846338|lt=Suwa}} * {{ill|Chino, Nagano|en|Chino, Nagano|WD=Q838660|lt=Chino}} * Suwa District: ** {{ill|Shimosuwa|en|Shimosuwa|WD=Q1204211|lt=Shimosuwa}} ** {{ill|Fujimi, Nagano|en|Fujimi, Nagano|WD=Q1204145|lt=Fujimi}} ** {{ill|Hara, Nagano|en|Hara, Nagano|WD=Q1203041|lt=Hara}}
The region can be broadly divided into two areas: - The "Lake Area" (west side) encompassing the three municipalities surrounding {{ill|Lake Suwa|en|Lake Suwa|WD=Q1206692|lt=Lake Suwa}}. - The "Yatsugatake Foothills" (east side), including Chino City, Hara Village, and Fujimi Town.
=== Geography === The Suwa Region is located approximately 200 km from Tokyo and Nagoya, connected by the Chūō Expressway and the JR Chūō Main Line. * '''Mountains''': {{ill|Mount Tateshina|en|Mount Tateshina|WD=Q717453|lt=Mount Tateshina}}, {{ill|Southern Yatsugatake Volcanic Group|en|Southern Yatsugatake Volcanic Group|WD=Q905588|lt=Yatsugatake}}, {{ill|Mount Kirigamine|en|Mount Kirigamine|WD=Q3695499|lt=Mount Kirigamine}} * '''Rivers''': Miyagawa River, Kamikawa River, Nakamongawa River * '''Lakes''': Lake Suwa, {{ill|Lake Shirakaba|ja|白樺湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Shirakaba|1=Lake Shirakaba|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579886}}, {{ill|Lake Tateshina|ja|蓼科湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Tateshina|1=Lake Tateshina|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621788}}
=== Climate === The region's climate varies significantly due to its topography. Areas near Lake Suwa tend to experience warmer summers and drier winters due to the Foehn effect, while the Yatsugatake Foothills are cooler in summer and have harsher winters, with climates akin to those of Hokkaido.
=== Economy === The Suwa Region has a history of silk production, later transitioning to a precision machinery industry. The {{ill|Suwa Area Industrial Messe|ja|諏訪圏工業メッセ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Area Industrial Messe|qq=}}, an industrial trade fair, highlights the area's manufacturing capabilities.
== History == === Ancient === The region's human activity dates back to the Jomon and Yayoi periods, with artifacts such as obsidian tools found at sites like Wada Pass.
=== Medieval to Early Modern Period === During the Sengoku period, the Suwa clan governed the region, with prominent figures like {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|en|Suwa Yorishige|WD=Q1213900|lt=Suwa Yorishige}}. After various conflicts, the clan was re-established under Suwa Yorimoto and later served the Tokugawa shogunate as lords of the Takashima Domain.
=== Modern === - 1871: Abolition of the domain system; incorporated into Takashima Prefecture and later Nagano Prefecture. - 1981: Chūō Expressway opens, improving regional connectivity.
== Tourism == The Suwa Region is a prominent tourist destination, drawing millions annually with attractions such as: - {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|WD=Q218813|lt=Suwa-taisha}}, a nationally renowned Shinto shrine complex. - {{ill|Takashima Castle|en|Takashima Castle|WD=Q10856395|lt=Takashima Castle}}, known as one of Japan’s three famous lakeside castles. - Numerous hot springs, including {{ill|Kamisuwa Onsen|ja|上諏訪温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisuwa Onsen|1=Kami-Suwa Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11360122}} and {{ill|Shimosuwa Onsen|ja|下諏訪温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosuwa Onsen|1=Shimosuwa Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361324}}.
=== Museums and Galleries === - {{ill|Kitazawa Museum of Art|ja|北澤美術館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitazawa Museum of Art|1=Kitazawa Museum of Art|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11403773}} - {{ill|Togariishi Museum of Jōmon Archaeology|en|Togariishi Museum of Jōmon Archaeology|WD=Q28154556|lt=Chino City Tateshina Archaeological Museum}}
=== Natural Attractions === - {{ill|Lake Suwa|en|Lake Suwa|WD=Q1206692|lt=Lake Suwa}} - {{ill|Mount Kirigamine|en|Mount Kirigamine|WD=Q3695499|lt=Mount Kirigamine}}
== Transportation == * '''Rail''': JR Chūō Main Line, with major stations such as Kamisuwa and Chino. * '''Road''': Chūō Expressway and Route 20, serving as primary thoroughfares.
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Suwa Area Federation|ja|諏訪広域連合|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Area Federation|1=Suwa Area Federation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11631871}}
== External Links == * [https://www.pref.nagano.lg.jp/koho/10koiki/suwa.html Official Suwa Region Information]
{{Region of Nagano Prefecture}}
{{translated page|ja|諏訪地域|version=101280331|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:諏訪地域]]

Except for Hara Village, most municipalities in the area are experiencing a population decline, with particularly severe decreases in {{ill|Shimosuwa|en|Shimosuwa|WD=Q1204211|lt=Shimosuwa}}, Okaya City, and Suwa City. The region is characterized by a multi-core structure centered on its three main cities, with Chino City being the most populous.
The {{ill|Chūō Main Line|en|Chūō Main Line|WD=Q1078110|lt=Chūō Main Line}} and {{ill|Chūō Expressway|en|Chūō Expressway|WD=Q1091276|lt=Chūō Expressway}} pass through the area, serving as key transport routes connecting {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō Region}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|en|Yamanashi Prefecture|WD=Q132720|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture}}, and the {{ill|Tōkai region|en|Tōkai region|WD=Q398989|lt=Tōkai Region}} while avoiding the Southern Alps.
== Overview == === Municipalities === The Suwa Region encompasses the following municipalities, stretching from west to east: * {{ill|Okaya, Nagano|en|Okaya, Nagano|WD=Q838672|lt=Okaya}} * {{ill|Suwa, Nagano|en|Suwa, Nagano|WD=Q846338|lt=Suwa}} * {{ill|Chino, Nagano|en|Chino, Nagano|WD=Q838660|lt=Chino}} * Suwa District: ** {{ill|Shimosuwa|en|Shimosuwa|WD=Q1204211|lt=Shimosuwa}} ** {{ill|Fujimi, Nagano|en|Fujimi, Nagano|WD=Q1204145|lt=Fujimi}} ** {{ill|Hara, Nagano|en|Hara, Nagano|WD=Q1203041|lt=Hara}}
The region can be broadly divided into two areas: - The "Lake Area" (west side) encompassing the three municipalities surrounding {{ill|Lake Suwa|en|Lake Suwa|WD=Q1206692|lt=Lake Suwa}}. - The "Yatsugatake Foothills" (east side), including Chino City, Hara Village, and Fujimi Town.
=== Geography === The Suwa Region is located approximately 200 km from Tokyo and Nagoya, connected by the Chūō Expressway and the JR Chūō Main Line. * '''Mountains''': {{ill|Mount Tateshina|en|Mount Tateshina|WD=Q717453|lt=Mount Tateshina}}, {{ill|Southern Yatsugatake Volcanic Group|en|Southern Yatsugatake Volcanic Group|WD=Q905588|lt=Yatsugatake}}, {{ill|Mount Kirigamine|en|Mount Kirigamine|WD=Q3695499|lt=Mount Kirigamine}} * '''Rivers''': Miyagawa River, Kamikawa River, Nakamongawa River * '''Lakes''': Lake Suwa, {{ill|Lake Shirakaba|ja|白樺湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Shirakaba|1=Lake Shirakaba|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579886}}, {{ill|Lake Tateshina|ja|蓼科湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Tateshina|1=Lake Tateshina|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621788}}
=== Climate === The region's climate varies significantly due to its topography. Areas near Lake Suwa tend to experience warmer summers and drier winters due to the Foehn effect, while the Yatsugatake Foothills are cooler in summer and have harsher winters, with climates akin to those of Hokkaido.
=== Economy === The Suwa Region has a history of silk production, later transitioning to a precision machinery industry. The {{ill|Suwa Area Industrial Messe|ja|諏訪圏工業メッセ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Area Industrial Messe|qq=}}, an industrial trade fair, highlights the area's manufacturing capabilities.
== History == === Ancient === The region's human activity dates back to the Jomon and Yayoi periods, with artifacts such as obsidian tools found at sites like Wada Pass.
=== Medieval to Early Modern Period === During the Sengoku period, the Suwa clan governed the region, with prominent figures like {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|en|Suwa Yorishige|WD=Q1213900|lt=Suwa Yorishige}}. After various conflicts, the clan was re-established under Suwa Yorimoto and later served the Tokugawa shogunate as lords of the Takashima Domain.
=== Modern === - 1871: Abolition of the domain system; incorporated into Takashima Prefecture and later Nagano Prefecture. - 1981: Chūō Expressway opens, improving regional connectivity.
== Tourism == The Suwa Region is a prominent tourist destination, drawing millions annually with attractions such as: - {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|WD=Q218813|lt=Suwa-taisha}}, a nationally renowned Shinto shrine complex. - {{ill|Takashima Castle|en|Takashima Castle|WD=Q10856395|lt=Takashima Castle}}, known as one of Japan’s three famous lakeside castles. - Numerous hot springs, including {{ill|Kamisuwa Onsen|ja|上諏訪温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisuwa Onsen|1=Kami-Suwa Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11360122}} and {{ill|Shimosuwa Onsen|ja|下諏訪温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosuwa Onsen|1=Shimosuwa Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361324}}.
=== Museums and Galleries === - {{ill|Kitazawa Museum of Art|ja|北澤美術館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitazawa Museum of Art|1=Kitazawa Museum of Art|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11403773}} - {{ill|Togariishi Museum of Jōmon Archaeology|en|Togariishi Museum of Jōmon Archaeology|WD=Q28154556|lt=Chino City Tateshina Archaeological Museum}}
=== Natural Attractions === - {{ill|Lake Suwa|en|Lake Suwa|WD=Q1206692|lt=Lake Suwa}} - {{ill|Mount Kirigamine|en|Mount Kirigamine|WD=Q3695499|lt=Mount Kirigamine}}
== Transportation == * '''Rail''': JR Chūō Main Line, with major stations such as Kamisuwa and Chino. * '''Road''': Chūō Expressway and Route 20, serving as primary thoroughfares.
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Suwa Area Federation|ja|諏訪広域連合|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Area Federation|1=Suwa Area Federation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11631871}}
== External Links == * [https://www.pref.nagano.lg.jp/koho/10koiki/suwa.html Official Suwa Region Information]

The region is characterized by a multi-core structure centered on its three main cities, with Chino City being the most populous.
The {{ill|Chūō Main Line|en|Chūō Main Line|WD=Q1078110|lt=Chūō Main Line|1=Chūō Main Line}} and {{ill|Chūō Expressway|en|Chūō Expressway|WD=Q1091276|lt=Chūō Expressway|1=Chūō Expressway}} pass through the area, serving as key transport routes connecting {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō Region|1=Kantō region}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|en|Yamanashi Prefecture|WD=Q132720|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture|1=Yamanashi Prefecture}}, and the {{ill|Tōkai region|en|Tōkai region|WD=Q398989|lt=Tōkai Region|1=Tōkai region}} while avoiding the Southern Alps.
== Overview == === Municipalities === The Suwa Region encompasses the following municipalities, stretching from west to east: * {{ill|Okaya, Nagano|en|Okaya, Nagano|WD=Q838672|lt=Okaya|1=Okaya, Nagano}} * {{ill|Suwa, Nagano|en|Suwa, Nagano|WD=Q846338|lt=Suwa|1=Suwa, Nagano}} * {{ill|Chino, Nagano|en|Chino, Nagano|WD=Q838660|lt=Chino|1=Chino, Nagano}} * Suwa District: ** {{ill|Shimosuwa|en|Shimosuwa|WD=Q1204211|lt=Shimosuwa|1=Shimosuwa}} ** {{ill|Fujimi, Nagano|en|Fujimi, Nagano|WD=Q1204145|lt=Fujimi|1=Fujimi, Nagano}} ** {{ill|Hara, Nagano|en|Hara, Nagano|WD=Q1203041|lt=Hara|1=Hara, Nagano}}
The region can be broadly divided into two areas: - The "Lake Area" (west side) encompassing the three municipalities surrounding {{ill|Lake Suwa|en|Lake Suwa|WD=Q1206692|lt=Lake Suwa|1=Lake Suwa}}. - The "Yatsugatake Foothills" (east side), including Chino City, Hara Village, and Fujimi Town.
=== Geography === The Suwa Region is located approximately 200 km from Tokyo and Nagoya, connected by the Chūō Expressway and the JR Chūō Main Line. * '''Mountains''': {{ill|Mount Tateshina|en|Mount Tateshina|WD=Q717453|lt=Mount Tateshina|1=Mount Tateshina}}, {{ill|Southern Yatsugatake Volcanic Group|en|Southern Yatsugatake Volcanic Group|WD=Q905588|lt=Yatsugatake|1=Southern Yatsugatake Volcanic Group}}, {{ill|Mount Kirigamine|en|Mount Kirigamine|WD=Q3695499|lt=Mount Kirigamine|1=Mount Kirigamine}} * '''Rivers''': Miyagawa River, Kamikawa River, Nakamongawa River * '''Lakes''': Lake Suwa, {{ill|Lake Shirakaba|ja|白樺湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Shirakaba|1=Lake Shirakaba|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579886}}, {{ill|Lake Tateshina|ja|蓼科湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Tateshina|1=Lake Tateshina|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621788}}
=== Climate === The region's climate varies significantly due to its topography. Areas near Lake Suwa tend to experience warmer summers and drier winters due to the Foehn effect, while the Yatsugatake Foothills are cooler in summer and have harsher winters, with climates akin to those of Hokkaido.
=== Economy === The Suwa Region has a history of silk production, later transitioning to a precision machinery industry. The {{ill|Suwa Area Industrial Messe|ja|諏訪圏工業メッセ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Area Industrial Messe|qq=}}, an industrial trade fair, highlights the area's manufacturing capabilities.
== History == === Ancient === The region's human activity dates back to the Jomon and Yayoi periods, with artifacts such as obsidian tools found at sites like Wada Pass.
=== Medieval to Early Modern Period === During the Sengoku period, the Suwa clan governed the region, with prominent figures like {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|en|Suwa Yorishige|WD=Q1213900|lt=Suwa Yorishige|1=Suwa Yorishige}}. After various conflicts, the clan was re-established under Suwa Yorimoto and later served the Tokugawa shogunate as lords of the Takashima Domain.
=== Modern === - 1871: Abolition of the domain system; incorporated into Takashima Prefecture and later Nagano Prefecture. - 1981: Chūō Expressway opens, improving regional connectivity.
== Tourism == The Suwa Region is a prominent tourist destination, drawing millions annually with attractions such as: - {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|WD=Q218813|lt=Suwa-taisha|1=Suwa-taisha}}, a nationally renowned Shinto shrine complex. - {{ill|Takashima Castle|en|Takashima Castle|WD=Q10856395|lt=Takashima Castle|1=Takashima Castle}}, known as one of Japan’s three famous lakeside castles. - Numerous hot springs, including {{ill|Kamisuwa Onsen|ja|上諏訪温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamisuwa Onsen|1=Kami-Suwa Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11360122}} and {{ill|Shimosuwa Onsen|ja|下諏訪温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosuwa Onsen|1=Shimosuwa Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361324}}.
=== Museums and Galleries === - {{ill|Kitazawa Museum of Art|ja|北澤美術館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitazawa Museum of Art|1=Kitazawa Museum of Art|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11403773}} - {{ill|Togariishi Museum of Jōmon Archaeology|en|Togariishi Museum of Jōmon Archaeology|WD=Q28154556|lt=Chino City Tateshina Archaeological Museum|1=Togariishi Museum of Jōmon Archaeology}}
=== Natural Attractions === - {{ill|Lake Suwa|en|Lake Suwa|WD=Q1206692|lt=Lake Suwa|1=Lake Suwa}} - {{ill|Mount Kirigamine|en|Mount Kirigamine|WD=Q3695499|lt=Mount Kirigamine|1=Mount Kirigamine}}
== Transportation == * '''Rail''': JR Chūō Main Line, with major stations such as Kamisuwa and Chino. * '''Road''': Chūō Expressway and Route 20, serving as primary thoroughfares.

{{Unreferenced|date=October 2019}}
'''Suwa Region''' (諏訪地域) refers to an area in the southern part of {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}}'s Minami-Shin region, centered around the cities of {{ill|Okaya, Nagano|en|Okaya, Nagano|WD=Q838672|lt=Okaya|1=Okaya, Nagano}}, {{ill|Suwa, Nagano|en|Suwa, Nagano|WD=Q846338|lt=Suwa|1=Suwa, Nagano}}, and {{ill|Chino, Nagano|en|Chino, Nagano|WD=Q838660|lt=Chino|1=Chino, Nagano}}. It is one of the ten administrative regions designated by the prefecture.<ref>[https://www.pref.nagano.lg.jp/koho/10koiki/suwa.html Information on the Suwa Region], Nagano Prefecture website (accessed August 3, 2018).</ref> This area corresponds closely to the former {{ill|Suwa District, Nagano|en|Suwa District, Nagano|WD=Q1131473|lt=Suwa District|1=Suwa District, Nagano}}.
As of 2019, the region's population was 194,439. Except for Hara Village, most municipalities in the area are experiencing a population decline, with particularly severe decreases in {{ill|Shimosuwa|en|Shimosuwa|WD=Q1204211|lt=Shimosuwa|1=Shimosuwa}}, Okaya City, and Suwa City. The region is characterized by a multi-core structure centered on its three main cities, with Chino City being the most populous.
The {{ill|Chūō Main Line|en|Chūō Main Line|WD=Q1078110|lt=Chūō Main Line|1=Chūō Main Line}} and {{ill|Chūō Expressway|en|Chūō Expressway|WD=Q1091276|lt=Chūō Expressway|1=Chūō Expressway}} pass through the area, serving as key transport routes connecting {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō Region|1=Kantō region}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|en|Yamanashi Prefecture|WD=Q132720|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture|1=Yamanashi Prefecture}}, and the {{ill|Tōkai region|en|Tōkai region|WD=Q398989|lt=Tōkai Region|1=Tōkai region}} while avoiding the Southern Alps.

The region can be broadly divided into two areas: - The "Lake Area" (west side) encompassing the three municipalities surrounding {{ill|Lake Suwa|en|Lake Suwa|WD=Q1206692|lt=Lake Suwa|1=Lake Suwa}}. - The "Yatsugatake Foothills" (east side), including Chino City, Hara Village, and Fujimi Town.
